concept_name: How Big Is the Circle? — r = mv/qB for a Charge in a Uniform Field
source_book: DC Pandey Class 12 Ch.4 Moving Charges and Magnetism — table-of-contents reference only (the 'circular motion of a charge in a uniform field' heading, §26.3 scope); teaching authored from first principles. NCERT consulted for the Indian real-world anchor only. This concept is the RADIUS / SIZE sibling of magnetic_force_moving_charge: it teaches that a charge moving perpendicular to a uniform magnetic field travels in a CLOSED circle whose radius is r = mv/qB — bigger with more momentum (m or v, the numerator), tighter with more grip (q or B, the denominator). The single surfaced quantity is r, and only as a RELATIVE readout (a yellow bar and 'wider/tighter than before' captions) — never a metres value. It deliberately does NOT teach the period T = 2 pi m / (q B) (that belongs to the future cyclotron_period_independent_of_speed), the force magnitude F = qvB sin(theta) (that belongs to magnetic_force_moving_charge), or any current-loop / Ampere / dipole content; no period, frequency, rev-per-sec, force magnitude, Newton or Tesla number ever reaches the canvas (hide_period_readout + hide_magnitude_readout on every state). Conceptual-only ship (no mode_overrides). EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + predict->reveal). Renders on field_3d via the radius_in_uniform_field scenario (the radius-only sibling of lorentz_force_uniform_field), which reuses the orbit basis + circle math and adds the four-factor R_visual scaling, the dashed radius line, the relative radius readout, and the ghost-compare frozen prior circle. Lighter atom by founder carve-out: no assessment block, no coverage_map.

real_world_anchor.primary: In a mass spectrometer, a lab tool used to separate the isotopes of an element, atoms are given the same speed and sent into the same magnet. A heavier atom carves out a WIDER circle than a lighter one, so the two land at different spots on the detector. The size of the circle literally reads out the atom's weight: r = mv/qB, so for the same speed, same charge and same field, a bigger mass means a bigger circle. Indian labs separate isotopes for medicine and research exactly this way.
real_world_anchor.secondary: At the VECC cyclotron in Kolkata, charged particles are whipped round and round through a uniform magnetic field. It is the same machine you met when learning that a magnetic force only steers — but now look at the SIZE of the loop. As the particles are sped up, their speed v rises, so r = mv/qB grows and the loop spirals outward into a wider and wider circle, exactly as the radius formula predicts.

m.name: Mass of the moving charge (numerator of r = mv/qB; bigger m = wider circle). Visual-scaling knob, the number is never shown on the canvas.

v.name: Speed of the moving charge (numerator of r = mv/qB; faster = wider circle). Visual-scaling knob, the number is never shown on the canvas.

q_mag.name: Magnitude of the charge (denominator of r = mv/qB; more charge = tighter circle). Visual-scaling knob, the number is never shown on the canvas.

B.name: Magnetic field strength (denominator of r = mv/qB; stronger field = tighter circle). Visual-scaling knob, the number is never shown on the canvas.

formulas.centripetal_balance: The perpendicular magnetic force IS the centripetal force: q v B = m v^2 / r
formulas.radius: Solve the balance for r: r = m v / (q B) — the only quantity this concept surfaces
formulas.numerator_is_momentum: The top of r = mv/qB is the momentum m times v: more momentum is harder to turn, so the circle is wider
formulas.denominator_is_grip: The bottom of r = mv/qB is the field's grip q times B: more grip turns it harder, so the circle is tighter

constraints: Teaches ONLY the radius r = m v / (q B) — the single surfaced quantity. Slider INPUT values for m, v, q, B are allowed; no other number reaches the canvas.
The radius readout is RELATIVE (wider or tighter than a ghost), never an absolute metres value, never a Newton or Tesla number.
NO period T = 2 pi m / (q B) anywhere — no T, no seconds-per-orbit, no rev-per-sec, no frequency f. Every state flags hide_period_readout: true.
NO force magnitude — no F = q v B, no q v B sin(theta), no Newton number. Every state inherits hide_magnitude_readout: true; the F arrow is a direction-only fixed glyph (Rule 29).
NO Ampere, NO current loop, NO magnetic dipole, NO solenoid.

The magnetic force is perpendicular to v at every instant, so it does no work, the speed |v| stays constant, and the path is a CLOSED circle (the why, inherited from the no-work sibling, not re-derived here).
The force points to ONE fixed centre with constant size, which is uniform circular motion: q v B = m v^2 / r.
Radius = momentum over grip: r grows with m or v (numerator), shrinks with q or B (denominator). A bigger B makes the circle TIGHTER, never bigger (this confronts the M1 misconception).
v makes a right angle with B throughout (perpendicular-entry only); the helical or oblique case is out of scope.

aha_moment.statement: More momentum widens the circle; more grip tightens it — size is mv over qB.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Raising B (then q) shrinks the live circle inside a frozen ghost, proving the denominator tightens the turn while the numerator (m, v) widened it in the state before.

STATE_1.title: It Doesn't Just Curve — It Closes

scene_composition.text: a charge crosses a uniform magnetic field — and its path is already curving

the curve bites its
own tail — the trail
snaps shut
it closes into a perfect circle — so how big is that circle?

tts_sentences.text_en: A charge moves through a uniform magnetic field — and its path is already curving.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक charge एक uniform magnetic field से होकर move करता है — और उसका path पहले से ही curving है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక charge ఒక uniform magnetic field గుండా move చేస్తుంది — మరియు దాని path ఇప్పటికే curving ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: If we let it keep going, where does this path end up?
tts_sentences.text_hi: अगर हम इसे आगे बढ़ने दें, तो यह path कहाँ जाकर खत्म होगा?
tts_sentences.text_te: మనం దీన్ని కొనసాగనిస్తే, ఈ path ఎక్కడ ముగుస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch — the curve bites its own tail. The trail snaps shut.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए — curve अपनी ही tail को काटता है। Trail बंद हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: చూడండి — curve దాని స్వంత tail ని కొరుకుతుంది. Trail మూసుకుపోతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: It closes into a perfect circle. So — how big is that circle?
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह एक perfect circle में बंद हो जाता है। तो — वह circle कितना बड़ा है?
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది ఒక perfect circle లోకి మూసుకుపోతుంది. కాబట్టి — ఆ circle ఎంత పెద్దది?

misconception_watch.belief: A magnetic field just bends the path a little; the charge eventually escapes in some open curve.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The trail completes a full loop and snaps shut on its starting point with a flash — a closed circle, not an open arc.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Perpendicular to a uniform field, the path is a CLOSED circle every time.

scene_composition.text: Predict: a same-size pull, always to one spot — what motion is that?

a radius line from
the centre stays the
SAME length all the
way round
a constant pull to one centre is uniform circular motion

tts_sentences.text_en: The force stays the same size and always points to one centre, while the charge orbits.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Force का size वही रहता है और charge के orbit करते समय, यह हमेशा एक centre की ओर point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Force అదే size లో ఉంటుంది మరియు charge orbit చేస్తున్నప్పుడు, ఇది ఎల్లప్పుడూ ఒక centre వైపు point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: A same-size pull, always to the same spot — what motion does that make?
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक same-size pull, हमेशा एक ही spot की ओर — इससे कौन सी motion बनती है?
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక same-size pull, ఎల్లప్పుడూ ఒకే spot వైపు — అది ఏ motion ని సృష్టిస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: A radius line from the centre — and it stays exactly the same length the whole way round.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Centre से एक radius line — और यह पूरे रास्ते में बिल्कुल same length का रहता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Centre నుండి ఒక radius line — మరియు అది మొత్తం మార్గంలో ఖచ్చితంగా same length లో ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: A constant centre-pull is the recipe for uniform circular motion.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक constant centre-pull, uniform circular motion के लिए recipe है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక constant centre-pull, uniform circular motion కోసం recipe.

misconception_watch.belief: The orbit is some egg-shape or spiral, not a true circle of one fixed size.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The dashed radius line rotates rigidly and never changes length, sweeping out one circle of a single fixed radius.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: A constant centre-pull keeps the radius fixed — it is a perfect circle.

STATE_3.title: The Magnetic Force IS the Centripetal Force

scene_composition.text: circular motion needs a centre force of size mv-squared over r

the magnetic force
IS the centre force
-> r = mv/qB

that one line is the
whole concept: the
circle's size is mv
over qB

tts_sentences.text_en: Circular motion needs a centre force of size m v-squared over r. The only force here is the magnetic one.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Circular motion को m v-squared over r size के centre force की ज़रूरत होती है। यहाँ एकमात्र force magnetic one है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Circular motion కి m v-squared over r size గల centre force అవసరం. ఇక్కడ ఉన్న ఏకైక force magnetic one.

tts_sentences.text_en: So what must be true about these two forces?
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो इन दो forces के बारे में क्या सच होना चाहिए?
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి ఈ రెండు forces గురించి ఏది నిజం కావాలి?

tts_sentences.text_en: They are the same force. The magnetic force IS the centripetal force: q v B equals m v-squared over r.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ये same force हैं। magnetic force ही centripetal force है: q v B equals m v-squared over r।
tts_sentences.text_te: అవి same force. magnetic force అనేది centripetal force: q v B equals m v-squared over r.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now just solve for r — the equation rearranges itself into r equals m v over q B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब बस r के लिए solve कीजिए — equation खुद को r equals m v over q B में rearrange कर लेती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు r కోసం solve చేయండి — equation తనంతటే r equals m v over q B గా rearrange అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That single line is the whole concept: the circle's size is m v over q B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वह single line ही पूरा concept है: circle का size, m v over q B है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆ single line అనేది మొత్తం concept: circle యొక్క size, m v over q B.

misconception_watch.belief: The circle's size is fixed by the apparatus, or it is arbitrary — there is no rule that sets it.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The balance q v B = m v-squared over r rearranges on screen into r = m v / q B, a single rule that fixes the size from m, v, q and B.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The radius is set exactly by m, v, q and B: r = mv/qB.

scene_composition.text: Predict: make the charge heavier — bigger circle, smaller, or the same?

a faint ghost marks
the old circle — the
live one swells past
it, twice
m and v are on top — more momentum mv is harder to turn

tts_sentences.text_en: m and v sit on top of the formula. Watch the circle as I make the charge heavier.
tts_sentences.text_hi: m और v formula के ऊपर हैं। देखिए, जैसे ही मैं charge को heavier बनाता हूँ, circle क्या होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: m మరియు v formula పై భాగంలో ఉన్నాయి. నేను charge ని heavier చేస్తున్నప్పుడు circle ని చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Make it heavier — bigger circle, smaller circle, or the same?
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसे heavier बनाइए — bigger circle, smaller circle, या same?
tts_sentences.text_te: దీన్ని heavier చేయండి — bigger circle, smaller circle, లేదా same?

tts_sentences.text_en: A faint ghost marks the old circle. With more mass, the live circle swells outward, past the ghost.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक faint ghost पुराने circle को mark करता है। ज़्यादा mass के साथ, live circle ghost से आगे बाहर की ओर swells होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక faint ghost పాత circle ని mark చేస్తుంది. ఎక్కువ mass తో, live circle ghost దాటి బయటకి swells అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Reset — now speed it up instead. Same story: it swells past a second ghost.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Reset — अब इसकी जगह इसे speed up कीजिए। Same story: यह एक second ghost से आगे swells हो जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Reset — ఇప్పుడు దీన్ని speed up చేయండి. Same story: ఇది ఒక second ghost దాటి swells అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Heavier or faster, both widen it — more momentum m v is just harder to turn.
tts_sentences.text_hi: heavier हो या faster, दोनों इसे widen करते हैं — ज़्यादा momentum m v को मोड़ना बस मुश्किल होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: heavier అయినా faster అయినా, రెండూ దీన్ని widen చేస్తాయి — ఎక్కువ momentum m v ని తిప్పడం కష్టంగా ఉంటుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: A heavier charge makes a tighter circle, because it is harder to move.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: When m is raised, the live circle swells OUTWARD past the frozen ghost — wider, not tighter.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: m is on top of r = mv/qB, so more mass means a WIDER circle.

misconception_watch.belief: Speeding the charge up does not change the size of the circle.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: When v is raised in the second reveal, the live circle swells past a second ghost, just as raising m did.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: v is on top too, so more speed also makes a WIDER circle.

STATE_5.title: More Charge or Stronger Field -> Tighter Circle

scene_composition.text: Predict: turn the field UP — does the circle get bigger, or tighter?

inside the ghost now
— a stronger field
grips harder, so the
circle shrinks

q and B are on the
bottom — qB is the
grip that tightens
the turn

tts_sentences.text_en: q and B sit on the bottom. Watch the circle as I turn the field UP.
tts_sentences.text_hi: q और B formula के नीचे हैं। देखिए, जैसे ही मैं field को UP करता हूँ, circle क्या होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: q మరియు B formula దిగువ భాగంలో ఉన్నాయి. నేను field ని UP చేస్తున్నప్పుడు circle ని చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Turn up the field — does the circle get bigger, or tighter?
tts_sentences.text_hi: field को UP कीजिए — क्या circle bigger होगा, या tighter?
tts_sentences.text_te: field ని UP చేయండి — circle bigger అవుతుందా, లేదా tighter అవుతుందా?

tts_sentences.text_en: Inside the ghost now — a stronger field grips harder, so the circle shrinks. Tighter, not bigger.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब ghost के inside — एक stronger field और harder grips करती है, इसलिए circle shrinks होता है। Tighter, bigger नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు ghost లోపల — ఒక stronger field మరింత harder గా grips చేస్తుంది, కాబట్టి circle shrinks అవుతుంది. Tighter, bigger కాదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Reset — add more charge instead. Same result: it shrinks again.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Reset — इसकी बजाय और charge add कीजिए। Same result: यह फिर से shrinks होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Reset — దీని బదులు ఇంకొక charge add చేయండి. Same result: అది మళ్ళీ shrinks అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: More field or more charge tightens it — q B is the grip, the bottom of the formula.
tts_sentences.text_hi: More field या more charge इसे tightens करता है — q B ही grip है, formula का bottom।
tts_sentences.text_te: More field లేదా more charge దీన్ని tightens చేస్తుంది — q B నే grip, formula యొక్క bottom.

misconception_watch.belief: A stronger magnetic field gives a BIGGER circle.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: When B is raised, the live circle shrinks INWARD, sitting inside the frozen ghost — tighter, never bigger.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: B is in the denominator of r = mv/qB, so more field means a TIGHTER circle.

misconception_watch.belief: More charge means more force, so the circle should get bigger.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: When q is raised in the second reveal, the live circle shrinks again, inside the ghost.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: q is in the denominator too: more charge grips harder and tightens the turn.

scene_composition.text: four sliders — m, v, q, B — and a live radius readout

call it out first —
bigger or smaller?
then check the
readout
top two widen it, bottom two tighten it — the readout follows r = mv/qB

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. Four sliders — m, v, q, B — and a live radius readout.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आपकी turn है। Four sliders — m, v, q, B — और एक live radius readout।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీ turn. Four sliders — m, v, q, B — మరియు ఒక live radius readout.

tts_sentences.text_en: Before you move one — call it out: will the circle get bigger or smaller? Then check.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आप एक को move करने से पहले — इसे call out कीजिए: क्या circle bigger होगा या smaller? फिर check कीजिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీరు ఒకదాన్ని move చేయడానికి ముందు — దీన్ని call out చేయండి: circle bigger అవుతుందా లేదా smaller అవుతుందా? తర్వాత check చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Top two widen it, bottom two tighten it. The readout follows r equals m v over q B exactly.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Top two इसे widen करते हैं, bottom two इसे tighten करते हैं। readout, r equals m v over q B को exactly follow करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Top two దీన్ని widen చేస్తాయి, bottom two దీన్ని tighten చేస్తాయి. readout, r equals m v over q B ని exactly follow చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Try cancelling — double m and double q, and the circle comes right back to its old size.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Cancelling try कीजिए — double m और double q, और circle अपनी old size पर वापस आ जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Cancelling try చేయండి — double m మరియు double q, మరియు circle దాని old size కి తిరిగి వస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: Changing the knobs changes the circle randomly; there is no single rule behind it.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Every slider edit resizes the live circle exactly as r = mv/qB predicts, and doubling m with q returns it to the same size.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The radius always obeys r = mv/qB — momentum on top, grip on the bottom.

STATE_1.label: It doesn't just curve — it closes

tts_sentences.text_en: A charge moves through a uniform magnetic field — and its path is already curving.
If we let it keep going, where does this path end up?
Watch — the curve bites its own tail. The trail snaps shut.
It closes into a perfect circle. So — how big is that circle?
The force stays the same size and always points to one centre, while the charge orbits.
A same-size pull, always to the same spot — what motion does that make?
A radius line from the centre — and it stays exactly the same length the whole way round.
A constant centre-pull is the recipe for uniform circular motion.

STATE_3.label: The magnetic force IS the centripetal force

tts_sentences.text_en: Circular motion needs a centre force of size m v-squared over r. The only force here is the magnetic one.
So what must be true about these two forces?
They are the same force. The magnetic force IS the centripetal force: q v B equals m v-squared over r.
Now just solve for r — the equation rearranges itself into r equals m v over q B.
That single line is the whole concept: the circle's size is m v over q B.
m and v sit on top of the formula. Watch the circle as I make the charge heavier.
Make it heavier — bigger circle, smaller circle, or the same?
A faint ghost marks the old circle. With more mass, the live circle swells outward, past the ghost.
Reset — now speed it up instead. Same story: it swells past a second ghost.
Heavier or faster, both widen it — more momentum m v is just harder to turn.

STATE_5.label: More charge or stronger field -> tighter circle

tts_sentences.text_en: q and B sit on the bottom. Watch the circle as I turn the field UP.
Turn up the field — does the circle get bigger, or tighter?
Inside the ghost now — a stronger field grips harder, so the circle shrinks. Tighter, not bigger.
Reset — add more charge instead. Same result: it shrinks again.
More field or more charge tightens it — q B is the grip, the bottom of the formula.
Your turn. Four sliders — m, v, q, B — and a live radius readout.
Before you move one — call it out: will the circle get bigger or smaller? Then check.
Top two widen it, bottom two tighten it. The readout follows r equals m v over q B exactly.
Try cancelling — double m and double q, and the circle comes right back to its old size.
